description: Having a modest view of one's importance; not proud or arrogant. 'Motu' names the quality of right-sized selfhood—seeing oneself clearly, neither inflated nor diminished.
sound_symbolism: The word settles low. 'Mo' grounds with quiet presence, ego released. Then 'tu' completes with simple clarity, humility at rest.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'motu phewo' (humble sage). Phi humility is not self-deprecation—it is accurate self-perception and openness to learning. Neighbor of 'thasu' (modest).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Humility arises naturally from understanding non-self—when the illusion of separate, superior selfhood dissolves.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional crafts taught humility—the material itself instructed, and mastery meant learning to follow rather than force.
